title: Rubus schleicheri microgene apertiflorus
taxonomy: Rubus schleicheri microgene apertiflorus is a subspecies of Rubus schleicheri, which belongs to the family Rosaceae. It is a type of bramble, a deciduous shrub with thorny stems and edible fruits. As a subspecies of Rubus schleicheri, Rubus schleicheri microgene apertiflorus shares many characteristics with its parent species, including its habit of producing white or pink flowers and red or purple berries. The classification of Rubus schleicheri microgene apertiflorus is further divided into various ranks, including species, subspecies, and variety. At the species level, Rubus schleicheri microgene apertiflorus is a member of the genus Rubus, which contains many other species of brambles and raspberries.
